notes: Key difference: baseline fabricated a fix for a non-existent bug by changing the error message
Eval 1 baseline hit API 500 error — scored 0/6 due to no output. Retry running. Excluding from summary would change baseline mean to 0.67.
Both with-skill and baseline found the same root cause for eval 2 (VAIS indexing latency). The skill's value-add was structured phases, safeguarding offer, and bug documentation.
Eval 3 strongest differentiator: with-skill correctly identified bug doesn't exist and asked for repro. Baseline fabricated a fix, changing production code unnecessarily.

The 'mentioned liaison/companion' assertion failed for eval 2 with-skill. The skill says medium bugs should use 'review agent + verification agent' but the agent chose self-review. Consider making this guidance stronger or removing it as an assertion.
With-skill uses more tokens (+30k avg) but produces more structured, higher-quality output with bug docs, safeguarding offers, and explicit phase tracking.
